result.markdown:
# Parametric hazard modeling with TemporalHazard

`TemporalHazard` is the R port of the SAS/C `HAZARD` module developed for cardiac-surgery outcomes research. It fits parametric multi-phase hazard models (the classic Blackstone early/constant/late decomposition) alongside the usual nonparametric estimators — Kaplan-Meier survival and the Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard. This chapter walks through the nonparametric estimates, a parametric Weibull fit, competing-risks cumulative incidence, and model diagnostics, plotting each result with `hvtiPlotR::theme_hv_manuscript()`.



We use the bundled `cabgkul` dataset throughout the survival/hazard sections: 5,880 patients with a follow-up time `int_dead` (interval to death, in years) and an event indicator `dead` (1 = death, 0 = censored). For the competing-risks section we switch to the bundled `omc` dataset, which records both thromboembolic events and death.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data(cabgkul, package = "TemporalHazard")
str(cabgkul)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
'data.frame':	5880 obs. of  2 variables:
 $ int_dead: num  201.83 195.06 7.13 126.36 187.57 ...
 $ dead    : int  0 0 1 1 0 0 1 1 0 1 ...
```


:::
:::


## Nonparametric estimates

`hzr_kaplan()` returns a data frame (class `hzr_kaplan`) with one row per event time and columns `time`, `n_risk`, `n_event`, `n_censor`, `survival`, `std_err`, `cl_lower`, `cl_upper`, `cumhaz`, `hazard`, `density`, and `life`. We plot the product-limit survival estimate with its logit-transformed confidence band.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
km <- hzr_kaplan(cabgkul$int_dead, cabgkul$dead)
head(km)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Kaplan-Meier estimate with logit confidence limits
Events: 69  | Time points: 6 
Survival range: 0.9883 to 0.9934 
RMST at last event: 0.1957 

    time n_risk n_event n_censor survival std_err cl_lower cl_upper cumhaz
 0.03285   5880      39        0   0.9934  0.0011   0.9909   0.9952 0.0067
 0.06571   5841       9        0   0.9918  0.0012   0.9892   0.9938 0.0082
 0.09856   5832       3        0   0.9913  0.0012   0.9886   0.9934 0.0087
 0.13142   5829       7        0   0.9901  0.0013   0.9873   0.9924 0.0099
 0.16427   5822       9        0   0.9886  0.0014   0.9855   0.9910 0.0115
 0.19713   5813       2        0   0.9883  0.0014   0.9852   0.9907 0.0118
 hazard density   life
 0.2026  0.2019 0.0328
 0.0469  0.0466 0.0655
 0.0157  0.0155 0.0981
 0.0366  0.0362 0.1306
 0.0471  0.0466 0.1632
 0.0105  0.0104 0.1957
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(km, aes(x = time, y = survival)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = cl_lower, ymax = cl_upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_step(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Survival probability",
       title = "Kaplan-Meier survival (cabgkul)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/km-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


`hzr_nelson()` returns the Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard (class `hzr_nelson`) with columns `time`, `n_risk`, `n_event`, `weight_sum`, `cumhaz`, `std_err`, `cl_lower`, `cl_upper`, `hazard`, and `cum_events`. We plot the cumulative hazard with its lognormal confidence limits.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
na <- hzr_nelson(cabgkul$int_dead, cabgkul$dead)
head(na)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Nelson cumulative hazard estimate with lognormal CL
Events: 69  | Time points: 6 

    time n_risk n_event weight_sum cumhaz std_err cl_lower cl_upper hazard
 0.03285   5880      39         39 0.0066   2e-04   0.0063   0.0070 0.2019
 0.06571   5841       9          9 0.0082   2e-04   0.0077   0.0087 0.0469
 0.09856   5832       3          3 0.0087   3e-04   0.0081   0.0093 0.0157
 0.13142   5829       7          7 0.0099   3e-04   0.0092   0.0106 0.0365
 0.16427   5822       9          9 0.0114   4e-04   0.0107   0.0122 0.0471
 0.19713   5813       2          2 0.0118   4e-04   0.0110   0.0126 0.0105
 cum_events
         39
         48
         51
         58
         67
         69
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(na, aes(x = time, y = cumhaz)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = cl_lower, ymax = cl_upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "firebrick") +
  geom_step(colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Cumulative hazard",
       title = "Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard (cabgkul)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/nelson-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


## Parametric hazard fit

`hazard()` fits a parametric model when `fit = TRUE`. Starting values for the parameter vector `theta` are supplied explicitly; for a Weibull the two parameters are the scale `mu` and the shape `nu`. The returned object (class `hazard`) is a list with elements `call`, `spec`, `data`, `fit`, `legacy_args`, and `engine`; the fitted parameters live in `fit$fit$theta`.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit <- hazard(time = cabgkul$int_dead, status = cabgkul$dead,
              theta = c(0.01, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE)
summary(fit)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
hazard model summary
  observations: 5880 
  predictors:   0 
  dist:         weibull 
  engine:       native-r-m2 
  converged:    TRUE 
  log-lik:      -3935.72 
  evaluations: fn=37, gr=9

Coefficients:
       estimate    std_error    z_stat       p_value
mu 0.0003613699 6.016407e-05  6.006407  1.896792e-09
nu 0.5944429760 2.346410e-02 25.334149 1.343733e-141
```


:::
:::


`predict.hazard()` evaluates the fitted model over a time grid. For `type = "survival"` and `type = "cumulative_hazard"` it returns a plain numeric vector aligned to the `time` column of `newdata`. (For an intercept-only model `type = "hazard"` returns the constant relative hazard `exp(eta) = 1`, so we derive the instantaneous hazard by differencing the cumulative hazard.)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
t_grid <- seq(0.5, max(cabgkul$int_dead), length.out = 120)
nd <- data.frame(time = t_grid)

pred <- data.frame(
  time     = t_grid,
  survival = predict(fit, newdata = nd, type = "survival"),
  cumhaz   = predict(fit, newdata = nd, type = "cumulative_hazard")
)
# Instantaneous hazard = d(cumhaz)/dt
pred$hazard <- c(NA, diff(pred$cumhaz) / diff(pred$time))
str(pred)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
'data.frame':	120 obs. of  4 variables:
 $ time    : num  0.5 2.19 3.88 5.58 7.27 ...
 $ survival: num  0.994 0.986 0.98 0.975 0.971 ...
 $ cumhaz  : num  0.00596 0.01434 0.02015 0.02498 0.02924 ...
 $ hazard  : num  NA 0.00495 0.00343 0.00286 0.00252 ...
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(pred, aes(x = time, y = survival)) +
  geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Survival probability",
       title = "Parametric (Weibull) survival") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/predict-survival-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(pred, aes(x = time, y = cumhaz)) +
  geom_line(colour = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Cumulative hazard",
       title = "Parametric (Weibull) cumulative hazard") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/predict-cumhaz-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(subset(pred, !is.na(hazard)), aes(x = time, y = hazard)) +
  geom_line(colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Instantaneous hazard rate",
       title = "Parametric (Weibull) hazard rate") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/predict-hazard-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


The fitted shape `nu < 1` produces the monotonically declining hazard typical of post-operative mortality risk that is highest early and then falls.

## Competing risks

`hzr_competing_risks()` estimates cumulative incidence functions for an integer-coded `event` vector (0 = censored, 1, 2, ... = competing event types). It returns a data frame (class `hzr_competing_risks`) with `time`, `n_risk`, per-type `n_event_k`, `n_censor`, overall event-free `surv`, per-type cumulative incidence `incid_k`, and standard errors `se_surv` / `se_k`.

The `omc` dataset records thromboembolic events (`te1`, `te2`, `te3`) and death (`dead`). We code a two-cause competing-risks outcome: type 1 = any thromboembolic event, type 2 = death.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data(omc, package = "TemporalHazard")
event <- with(omc, ifelse(te1 == 1 | te2 == 1 | te3 == 1, 1L,
                          ifelse(dead == 1, 2L, 0L)))
table(event)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
event
  0   1   2 
257  33  49 
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cr <- hzr_competing_risks(omc$int_dead, event)
head(cr)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Competing risks cumulative incidence
Event types: 2  | Time points: 6 
Final survival: 0.982 
Final incid_1 : 0.0059 
Final incid_2 : 0.012 

     time n_risk n_event_1 n_event_2 n_censor   surv incid_1 incid_2 se_surv
  0.06571    339         1         0        0 0.9971  0.0029   0.000  0.0029
  6.93238    334         0         1        0 0.9941  0.0029   0.003  0.0042
  8.37800    333         0         1        0 0.9911  0.0029   0.006  0.0051
  8.87082    331         1         0        0 0.9881  0.0059   0.006  0.0059
 16.85456    326         0         1        0 0.9851  0.0059   0.009  0.0066
 16.98598    325         0         1        0 0.9820  0.0059   0.012  0.0073
   se_1   se_2
 0.0029 0.0000
 0.0029 0.0030
 0.0029 0.0042
 0.0042 0.0042
 0.0042 0.0052
 0.0042 0.0060
```


:::
:::


We reshape the two incidence columns to long form and plot both cumulative incidence curves.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cif <- data.frame(
  time      = rep(cr$time, 2),
  incidence = c(cr$incid_1, cr$incid_2),
  cause     = factor(rep(c("Thromboembolic", "Death"), each = nrow(cr)))
)

ggplot(cif, aes(x = time, y = incidence, colour = cause)) +
  geom_step(linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Cumulative incidence",
       colour = "Cause", title = "Competing-risks cumulative incidence (omc)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/cr-plot-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


## Diagnostics

`hzr_gof()` compares the fitted parametric model against the Kaplan-Meier estimate. With its default time grid (the observed event times) it returns columns `time`, `n_risk`, `n_event`, `n_censor`, `km_surv`, `km_cumhaz`, `par_surv`, `par_cumhaz`, `cum_observed`, and `cum_expected`, plus a `residual` (expected minus observed). A `"summary"` attribute carries the scalar conservation diagnostics.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
gof <- hzr_gof(fit)
attr(gof, "summary")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$total_observed
[1] 545

$total_expected
[1] 544.97

$final_residual
[1] -0.03001163

$dist
[1] "weibull"

$n
[1] 5880
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
head(gof)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. expected events
Distribution: weibull  | n = 5880 

Total observed events: 545 
Total expected events: 544.97 
Final residual (E - O): -0.03 
Conservation ratio (E/O): 1 

Use plot columns: time, km_surv, par_surv, cum_observed, cum_expected, residual
```


:::
:::


Plotting the parametric survival against the Kaplan-Meier survival shows how closely the Weibull tracks the empirical curve.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
gof_long <- data.frame(
  time     = rep(gof$time, 2),
  survival = c(gof$km_surv, gof$par_surv),
  model    = factor(rep(c("Kaplan-Meier", "Parametric"), each = nrow(gof)))
)

ggplot(gof_long, aes(x = time, y = survival, colour = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Survival probability",
       colour = NULL, title = "Goodness of fit: observed vs. parametric") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/gof-plot-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


The cumulative observed versus expected events curve is the survival-analysis analogue of a calibration plot; close agreement (a conservation ratio near 1) indicates the parametric model reproduces the event count over time.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
oe <- data.frame(
  time   = rep(gof$time, 2),
  events = c(gof$cum_observed, gof$cum_expected),
  kind   = factor(rep(c("Observed", "Expected"), each = nrow(gof)))
)

ggplot(oe, aes(x = time, y = events, colour = kind)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  labs(x = "Years since operation", y = "Cumulative events",
       colour = NULL, title = "Cumulative observed vs. expected events") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/gof-oe-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::


Finally, `hzr_calibrate()` bins a continuous covariate into quantile groups and reports the observed event probability per bin. It returns columns `group`, `n`, `events`, `mean`, `min`, `max`, `prob`, and `link_value` (the event probability on the chosen link scale). Here we calibrate against follow-up time itself as a simple worked example.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cal <- hzr_calibrate(cabgkul$int_dead, cabgkul$dead, groups = 10)
cal
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Variable calibration (logit link, 10 groups)

 group   n events    mean     min     max  prob link_value
     1 588    178   5.459   0.033  10.251 0.303     -0.834
     2 588     33  14.284  10.251  18.629 0.056     -2.822
     3 588     35  23.803  18.694  28.682 0.060     -2.760
     4 588     37  34.431  28.682  40.083 0.063     -2.701
     5 588     49  46.693  40.182  52.404 0.083     -2.398
     6 588     29  57.231  52.404  61.504 0.049     -2.959
     7 588     37  67.096  61.537  73.726 0.063     -2.701
     8 588     44  82.006  73.726  91.107 0.075     -2.515
     9 588     37 101.465  91.107 114.171 0.063     -2.701
    10 588     66 136.711 114.171 201.828 0.112     -2.068
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(cal, aes(x = mean, y = prob)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(colour = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "Mean follow-up time in bin (years)", y = "Observed event probability",
       title = "Calibration across follow-up-time deciles") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](temporal_hazard_files/figure-pdf/calibrate-1.pdf){fig-pos='H'}
:::
:::
